isolation.query: LeftRegion(s, region) :- destrieux(s, region) & startswith("L S", s)

assertions.title: Click VBR result to overlay

steps.observed: App loaded, NeuroLang Sparklis title shown

steps.expected: Destrieux engine selected, atlas loads in BrainViewer
steps.observed: Destrieux selected, BrainViewer shows 3-slice MNI atlas

steps.action: Set query: LeftRegion(s, region) :- destrieux(s, region) & startswith("L S", s)
steps.observed: Query set via React fiber state dispatch (CodeMirror API)

steps.expected: Results table shows 31 rows with VBR badge column and 'View in brain' buttons
steps.observed: Results table shows 31 rows with VBR badge column, BUT 'View in brain' button (🧠) is ABSENT. Action column cells are empty.

steps.expected: Button condition evaluates true for VBR rows
steps.observed: BUG: DataTable button condition checks `typeof C === 'string'` where C is the VBR column value. The API now returns VBR values as objects {min, max, q95, image, hash, center, idx} instead of raw base64 strings. The check fails, so no button is rendered.

evidence.network: WebSocket query returns ExplicitVBR data as objects with {min, max, q95, image, hash, center, idx}. row_type: ["<class 'str'>", "<class 'neurolang.regions.ExplicitVBR'>"]

assertions.issues: BUG: The DataTable component (function Tk) checks `typeof S === 'string' && S.length > 0` to determine whether to show the 'View in brain' button. The VBR column value is now an object {min, max, q95, image, hash, center, idx} (image field is a base64 string of length ~2.9M). The typeof check fails, so the button is never rendered. Fix: change the condition to `(typeof S === 'string' && S.length > 0) || (typeof S === 'object' && S !== null && typeof S.image === 'string' && S.image.length > 0)` and update the addOverlay call to use `S.image` or `S` depending on the actual type.

assertions.title: Probability overlay with color bar

steps.action: Attempt to find ExplicitVBROverlay data via agg_create_region_overlay query
steps.expected: Results table shows VBROverlay cells with View in brain buttons
steps.observed: Cannot test via standard flow because VAL-BRAIN-002 'View in brain' button is not rendered (see VAL-BRAIN-002 bug)

steps.action: Manually inject probabilistic overlay via React context addOverlay({isProbabilistic: true, colormap: 'hot', ...})
steps.expected: Color bar appears in overlay manager for probabilistic overlay; colored overlay renders in Niivue canvas
steps.observed: Color bar DOES appear in overlay manager (data-testid='color-bar' and 'color-bar-gradient' present, showing gradient from 0 to 1). HOWEVER the Niivue canvas does NOT show the colored overlay due to a secondary bug: BrainViewer tries setOverlayList (doesn't exist in Niivue 0.43.7), then falls back to addOverlay loop (also doesn't exist), with all TypeErrors silently swallowed.

evidence.consoleErrors: niivue-warn: 100% of voxels are zero: ignoring zeros for cal_max (from NVImage.loadFromBase64 on the VBR data). No 'Failed to sync overlays' warning because addOverlay exceptions are silently swallowed in individual try/catch blocks.
evidence.network: No network requests for overlay loading - base64 data passed directly to Niivue

assertions.issues: TWO BUGS: (1) Cannot trigger overlay via UI (see VAL-BRAIN-002). (2) Even when overlay is injected directly via context, Niivue canvas does not display it. BrainViewer code tries `r.current.setOverlayList(b)` (not in Niivue 0.43.7), then falls back to `r.current.addOverlay(A)` per overlay (also not in Niivue 0.43.7). Both methods throw TypeError caught silently. The correct Niivue 0.43.7 API for adding overlays is `nv.addVolume(nvImage)`. PARTIAL PASS: The color bar UI element in the OverlayManager sidebar correctly appears with probability scale (0-1) and hot colormap gradient when isProbabilistic=true.

assertions.title: Add multiple overlays

steps.action: Manually add two overlays via React context addOverlay (bypassing broken 'View in brain' button)
steps.expected: Overlay manager shows 2 items; both overlays visible in Niivue canvas
steps.observed: Overlay manager correctly shows 'Overlays (2)' with both overlay names and remove buttons. Niivue canvas does NOT show either overlay (same Niivue API bug as VAL-BRAIN-003).

assertions.issues: Cannot test via standard user flow because 'View in brain' button is not rendered (VAL-BRAIN-002 bug). Even when overlays are manually added via context, they do not appear in the Niivue canvas. PARTIAL PASS: The OverlayManager UI correctly tracks and displays multiple overlays with the 'Overlays (N)' header updating correctly.

assertions.title: Remove overlay

steps.action: With 2 overlays in overlay manager, click ✕ remove button (data-testid='overlay-remove-btn') on first overlay
steps.expected: First overlay removed; overlay manager shows 'Overlays (1)' with only second overlay
steps.observed: Remove button click works: 'Overlays (2)' becomes 'Overlays (1)', only second overlay remains. PASS for UI state management.

steps.action: Remove the second and final overlay via remove button
steps.expected: Overlay manager disappears (no overlays); brain viewer shows base atlas only
steps.observed: Overlay manager disappears entirely when last overlay is removed. Brain viewer continues showing base atlas. PASS for UI state management.

steps.action: Verify brain canvas response to overlay removal
steps.expected: Colored overlays disappear from brain canvas
steps.observed: N/A - overlays were never displayed in canvas due to Niivue API bug, so no change visible in canvas

assertions.issues: PARTIAL FAIL: The OverlayManager remove button (data-testid='overlay-remove-btn') and 'Clear all' button (data-testid='overlay-clear-all-btn') both work correctly for UI state management. Overlay count updates, items are removed from the list, and the manager disappears when empty. However: (1) Overlays cannot be added via normal user interaction (see VAL-BRAIN-002 bug - no 'View in brain' button), so the full user flow cannot be completed. (2) Since Niivue overlays never loaded, there is nothing to remove from the canvas (secondary issue). The remove/clear UI mechanics are correctly implemented.

frictions.description: The 'View in brain' button is completely absent from the results table because DataTable checks `typeof C === 'string'` but VBR data is now returned as an object {min, max, q95, image, hash, center, idx}. This is a critical regression that blocks all overlay-related user flows.
frictions.resolution: Not fixed - this is a code bug to be reported

frictions.description: BrainViewer uses Niivue methods setOverlayList and addOverlay that do not exist in Niivue 0.43.7. The correct API is addVolume(nvImage). Exceptions are silently swallowed, so no user-visible error appears.
frictions.resolution: Not fixed - this is a code bug to be reported

frictions.description: CodeMirror editor content cannot be set via standard fill/keyboard input in agent-browser. The editor content had to be set via React fiber state dispatch. This required deep exploration of the React fiber tree to find the right state queue.
frictions.resolution: Used React fiber state dispatch: found component 'qv' in fiber tree, navigated to si:2 (string state), called queue.dispatch(newQuery)

frictions.description: The 'View in brain' button test required bypassing via direct React context API calls (overlayCtx.addOverlay) to test overlay manager UI for VAL-BRAIN-003, 004, 005.
frictions.resolution: Found overlay context provider in React fiber tree at depth 11, used addOverlay/clearOverlays functions directly

summary: Tested 4 assertions: 0 passed, 4 failed. Two critical bugs found: (1) DataTable 'View in brain' button never appears because VBR column values are objects {min,max,q95,image,...} but the button condition checks `typeof value === 'string'`; (2) BrainViewer overlay sync uses Niivue methods (setOverlayList, addOverlay) that don't exist in Niivue 0.43.7, so overlays never appear in the canvas. Partial functionality confirmed: the OverlayManager UI correctly tracks overlay state (add/remove/count), color bar renders for isProbabilistic overlays, and remove buttons work. But the full end-to-end flow (click row → see colored overlay in brain viewer) is completely broken due to bugs 1 and 2.
